revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: 新しくページを書きました。
revision.text:
'''等乃伎神社'''（とのきじんじゃ、とのぎじんじゃ）は、[[高石市]]の北東部、取石２丁目にある、[[神社]]である<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html|title=等乃伎神社ホームページ|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == [[天児屋根命]]
[[大歳神]]・壺大神・[[菅原道真]]・[[誉田別命]]（[[応神天皇]]）
[[摂社]]…[[宇賀之御魂神]]（[[お稲荷さん]]）
[[末社]]…[[天御中主神]]
== 由緒 == [[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]。
[[1908年]]（明治41年）～[[1909年]]（明治42年）、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を、[[合祀]]。
== 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。
== 文化財 == 本殿・拝殿は、昭和62年に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。
境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。
境内地からは、[[南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。
== 所在地 == 高石市取石2丁目14－48。
最寄駅は、[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]。
== 脚注 ==

revision.comment: /* 由緒 */ 出典を明記しました。

[[1908年]]（明治41年）～[[1909年]]（明治42年）、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を、[[合祀]]。
== 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。
== 文化財 == 本殿・拝殿は、昭和62年に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。
境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。
境内地からは、[[南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。
== 所在地 == 高石市取石2丁目14－48。
最寄駅は、[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]。
== 脚注 == <references /> [[Category:和泉国の式内社|とのきじんじゃ]]

== 祭神 == 主神は、[[天児屋根命]]
[[大歳神]]・壺大神・[[菅原道真]]・[[誉田別命]]（[[応神天皇]]）
[[摂社]]…[[宇賀之御魂神]]（[[お稲荷さん]]）
[[末社]]…[[天御中主神]]
== 由緒 == [[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37?tocOpened=1|title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション『延喜式巻九・神祇九』|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}</ref>
[[1908年]]（明治41年）～[[1909年]]（明治42年）、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を、[[合祀]]。
== 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。
従来はこの日とその前後に、氏子地域（旧富木村・旧新家村・旧土生村）の秋祭りのだんじりが宮入りしていた。現在は、祭りを休日に設定しようという努力により、10月の第一土曜日または第二土曜日に宮入りとなっている。
== 文化財 == 本殿・拝殿は、昭和62年に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。
境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。
境内地からは、[[南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。
== 所在地 == 高石市取石2丁目14－48。
最寄駅は、[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]。
== 脚注 == <references /> [[Category:和泉国の式内社|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:大阪府の神社|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:高石市|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:高石市の建築物|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:高石市の歴史|とのきじんじゃ]]

'''等乃伎神社'''（とのきじんじゃ、とのぎじんじゃ）は、[[高石市]]の北東部、取石２丁目にある、[[神社]]である<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html|title=等乃伎神社ホームページ|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}</ref>。

[[1908年]]（明治41年）～[[1909年]]（明治42年）、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を、[[合祀]]。
== 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。
従来はこの日とその前後に、[[高石だんじり祭]]のうち、氏子地域（旧富木村・旧新家村・旧土生村）の秋祭りのだんじりが宮入りしていた。現在は、祭りを休日に設定しようという努力により、10月の第一土曜日または第二土曜日に宮入りとなっている。
== 文化財 == 本殿・拝殿は、昭和62年に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。
境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。
境内地からは、[[南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。
== 所在地 == 高石市取石2丁目14－48。
最寄駅は、[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]。

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=等乃伎神社 |画像= |所在地=[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石2丁目14‐48 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 16.061 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 27 | 経度秒 = 23.504 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[天児屋根命]] |社格=[[式内社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= 例祭（[[10月5日]]） |神事= }} '''等乃伎神社'''（とのきじんじゃ、とのぎじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石にある[[神社]]{{Cite web|url=http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html|title=等乃伎神社ホームページ|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主祭神 * [[天児屋根命]]（アマノコヤネノミコト） ; 相殿神 * [[誉田別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） * [[菅原道真]]（スガワラノミチザネ） * [[大歳神]] * 壺大神 == 歴史 == 「等乃伎」という名称は、かつてこの地を収めていた[[中臣氏]]の一支族である殿来連から由来している。[[続日本紀]]の[[天平勝宝]]4年（752年）条に「中臣殿来連竹田売」という人物の名前が残っており、その頃に領地を治めていた中臣殿来連竹田売が祖先である天児屋根命を祀ったことから始まりとされる。また、同年には[[太政大臣]]である[[藤原武智麻呂]]、その子の[[大納言]]である[[恵美押勝]]（藤原仲麻呂)が相次いでこの地へ赴き、居住していたとされている[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671　等乃伎神社の情報｜ 神社・お寺御朱印検索No.1／神社がいいね]2018年6月22日　閲覧。[[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]とされている{{Cite web|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37?tocOpened=1|title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション『延喜式巻九・神祇九』|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。[[1908年]]（明治41年）から翌年[[1909年]]（明治42年）にかけて、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を[[合祀]]した。 == 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。従来はこの日とその前後に、[[高石だんじり祭]]のうち、氏子地域（旧富木村・旧新家村・旧土生村）の秋祭りのだんじりが宮入りしていた。現在は、祭りを休日に設定しようという努力により、10月の第一土曜日または第二土曜日に宮入りとなっている。 == 境内 == 本殿・拝殿は、昭和62年に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。 境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。境内地からは、[[南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。 == 境内社 == [[摂社]]…[[お稲荷さん|宇賀之御魂神]] [[末社]]…[[天御中主神]] == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石2丁目14‐48 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600m） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html　等乃伎神社（公式サイト）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とのきしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:高石市|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:高石市の建築物|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:高石市の歴史|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社|とのきじんじゃ]]

{{神社 |名称=等乃伎神社 |画像=[[File:Tonoki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石2丁目14‐48 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 16.061 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 27 | 経度秒 = 23.504 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[天児屋根命]] |社格=[[式内社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= 例祭（[[10月5日]]） |神事= }} '''等乃伎神社'''（とのきじんじゃ、とのぎじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石にある[[神社]]{{Cite web|url=http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html|title=等乃伎神社ホームページ|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主祭神 * [[天児屋根命]]（アマノコヤネノミコト） ; 相殿神 * [[誉田別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） * [[菅原道真]]（スガワラノミチザネ） * [[大歳神]] * 壺大神 == 歴史 == 「等乃伎」という名称は、かつてこの地を収めていた[[中臣氏]]の一支族である殿来連から由来している。[[続日本紀]]の[[天平勝宝]]4年（752年）条に「中臣殿来連竹田売」という人物の名前が残っており、その頃に領地を治めていた中臣殿来連竹田売が祖先である天児屋根命を祀ったことから始まりとされる。また、同年には[[太政大臣]]である[[藤原武智麻呂]]、その子の[[大納言]]である[[恵美押勝]]（藤原仲麻呂)が相次いでこの地へ赴き、居住していたとされている[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671　等乃伎神社の情報｜ 神社・お寺御朱印検索No.1／神社がいいね]2018年6月22日　閲覧。[[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]とされている{{Cite web|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37?tocOpened=1|title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション『延喜式巻九・神祇九』|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。[[1908年]]（明治41年）から翌年[[1909年]]（明治42年）にかけて、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を[[合祀]]した。 == 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。従来はこの日とその前後に、[[高石だんじり祭]]のうち、氏子地域（旧富木村・旧新家村・旧土生村）の秋祭りのだんじりが宮入りしていた。現在は、祭りを休日に設定しようという努力により、10月の第一土曜日または第二土曜日に宮入りとなっている。 == 境内 == 本殿・拝殿は、昭和62年に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。 境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。境内地からは、[[南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。 == 境内社 == [[摂社]]…[[お稲荷さん|宇賀之御魂神]] [[末社]]…[[天御中主神]] == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石2丁目14‐48 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600m） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html　等乃伎神社（公式サイト）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とのきしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:高石市|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:高石市の建築物|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:高石市の歴史|とのきじんじゃ]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社|とのきじんじゃ]]

旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= 例祭（[[10月5日]]） |神事= }} '''等乃伎神社'''（とのきじんじゃ、とのぎじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石にある[[神社]]{{Cite web|url=http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html|title=等乃伎神社ホームページ|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主祭神 * [[天児屋根命]]（アメノコヤネノミコト） ; 相殿神 * [[誉田別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） * [[菅原道真]]（スガワラノミチザネ） * [[大歳神]] * 壺大神 == 歴史 == 当神社の案内板によれば、「等乃伎」という名称はかつてこの地を収めていた[[中臣氏]]の一支族である殿来連から由来し、[[続日本紀]]の[[天平勝宝]]4年（752年）条に「中臣殿来連竹田売」という人物の名前が残っており、その頃に領地を治めていた中臣殿来連竹田売が祖先である天児屋根命を祀ったことから始まりということになっており、また同年には[[太政大臣]]である[[藤原武智麻呂]]、その子の[[大納言]]である[[恵美押勝]]（藤原仲麻呂)が相次いでこの地へ赴き、居住していたとされている[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671　等乃伎神社の情報｜ 神社・お寺御朱印検索No.1／神社がいいね]2018年6月22日　閲覧。 しかし、この伝承と当神社の関連については定かでない。 [[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]とされている{{Cite web|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37?tocOpened=1|title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション『延喜式巻九・神祇九』|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。 [[1908年]]（明治41年）から翌年[[1909年]]（明治42年）にかけて、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を[[合祀]]した。 == 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。 従来はこの日とその前後に、[[高石だんじり祭]]のうち、氏子地域（旧富木村・旧新家村・旧土生村・旧綾井村・旧大園村）の秋祭りのだんじりが宮入りしていた。 現在は、祭りを休日に設定しようという努力により、10月の第一土曜日または第二土曜日に宮入りとなっている。 == 境内 == 本殿・拝殿は、昭和62年に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。 境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。境内地からは、[[南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。 == 境内社 == [[摂社]]…[[お稲荷さん|宇賀之御魂神]] [[末社]]…[[天御中主神]] == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石2丁目14‐48 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600m） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html　等乃伎神社（公式サイト）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とのきしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高石市の建築物]] [[Category:高石市の歴史]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称=等乃伎神社 |画像=[[File:Tonoki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石二丁目14番48号 | ISO = JP-27 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 16.061 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 27 | 経度秒 = 23.504 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神=[[天児屋根命]] |社格=[[式内社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= 例祭（[[10月5日]]） |神事= | 地図 = Japan Osaka }} '''等乃伎神社'''（とのきじんじゃ、とのぎじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石にある[[神社]]{{Cite web|url=http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html|title=等乃伎神社ホームページ|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 [[ファイル:Tonogi-jinja.jpg |サムネイル|等乃伎神社の鳥居]] ==祭神== ; 主祭神 * [[天児屋根命]]（アメノコヤネノミコト） ; 相殿神 * [[誉田別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） * [[菅原道真]]（スガワラノミチザネ） * [[大歳神]] * 壺大神 == 歴史 == 当神社の案内板によれば、「等乃伎」という名称はかつてこの地を収めていた[[中臣氏]]の一支族である殿来連から由来し、[[続日本紀]]の[[天平勝宝]]4年（752年）条に「中臣殿来連竹田売」という人物の名前が残っており、その頃に領地を治めていた中臣殿来連竹田売が祖先である天児屋根命を祀ったことから始まりということになっており、また同年には[[太政大臣]]である[[藤原武智麻呂]]、その子の[[大納言]]である[[恵美押勝]]（藤原仲麻呂)が相次いでこの地へ赴き、居住していたとされている[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671　等乃伎神社の情報｜ 神社・お寺御朱印検索No.1／神社がいいね]2018年6月22日　閲覧。 しかし、この伝承と当神社の関連については定かでない。 [[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]とされている{{Cite web|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37?tocOpened=1|title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション『延喜式巻九・神祇九』|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。 [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）から翌年[[1909年]]（明治42年）にかけて、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を[[合祀]]した。 == 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。 従来はこの日とその前後に、[[高石だんじり祭]]のうち、氏子地域（旧富木村・旧新家村・旧土生村・旧綾井村・旧大園村）の秋祭りのだんじりが宮入りしていた。 現在は、祭りを休日に設定しようという努力により、10月の第一土曜日または第二土曜日に宮入りとなっている。 == 境内 == 本殿・拝殿は、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。 境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。境内地からは、[[南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。 == 境内社 == [[摂社]]…[[お稲荷さん|宇賀之御魂神]] [[末社]]…[[天御中主神]] == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石二丁目14番48号 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600[[メートル|m]]） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * [[大阪府の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{osm box|w|562122660}} * [http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html　等乃伎神社（公式サイト）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とのきしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高石市の建築物]] [[Category:高石市の歴史]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

revision.text:

{{神社 |名称=等乃伎神社 |画像=[[File:Tonoki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord2|34|31|16.061|N|135|27|23.504|E}}}} |所在地=[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石二丁目14番48号 | ISO = JP-27 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 16.061 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 27 | 経度秒 = 23.504 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神=[[天児屋根命]] |社格=[[式内社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= 例祭（[[10月5日]]） |神事= | 地図 = Japan Osaka }} '''等乃伎神社'''（とのきじんじゃ、とのぎじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石にある[[神社]]{{Cite web|url=http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html|title=等乃伎神社ホームページ|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 [[ファイル:Tonogi-jinja.jpg |サムネイル|等乃伎神社の鳥居]] == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[天児屋根命]]（アマノコヤネノミコト、アメノコヤネノミコト） ; 相殿神 * [[誉田別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） * [[菅原道真]]（スガワラノミチザネ） * [[大歳神]] * 壺大神 == 歴史 == 当神社の案内板によれば、「等乃伎」という名称はかつてこの地を収めていた[[中臣氏]]の一支族である殿来連から由来し、[[続日本紀]]の[[天平勝宝]]4年（752年）条に「中臣殿来連竹田売」という人物の名前が残っており、その頃に領地を治めていた中臣殿来連竹田売が祖先である天児屋根命を祀ったことから始まりということになっており、また同年には[[太政大臣]]である[[藤原武智麻呂]]、その子の[[大納言]]である[[恵美押勝]]（藤原仲麻呂)が相次いでこの地へ赴き、居住していたとされている[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671　等乃伎神社の情報｜ 神社・お寺御朱印検索No.1／神社がいいね]2018年6月22日　閲覧。 [[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]とされている{{Cite web|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37?tocOpened=1|title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション『延喜式巻九・神祇九』|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。 [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）から翌年[[1909年]]（明治42年）にかけて、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を[[合祀]]した。 == 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。 従来はこの日とその前後に、[[高石だんじり祭]]のうち、氏子地域（旧富木村・旧新家村・旧土生村・旧綾井村・旧大園村）の秋祭りのだんじりが宮入りしていた。 現在は、祭りを休日に設定しようという努力により、10月の第一土曜日または第二土曜日に宮入りとなっている。 == 境内 == 本殿・拝殿は、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。 境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。境内地からは、[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。 == 境内社 == [[摂社]]…[[お稲荷さん|宇賀之御魂神]] [[末社]]…[[天御中主神]] == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石二丁目14番48号 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600[[メートル|m]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * [[大阪府の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{osm box|w|562122660}} * [http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html　等乃伎神社（公式サイト）] * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai10sibu/takaisi-city/m26_02_takaisi_tonogi.html 等乃伎神社] - （大阪府神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とのきしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高石市の建築物]] [[Category:高石市の歴史]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称=等乃伎神社 |画像=[[File:Tonoki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]

{{神社 |名称=等乃伎神社 |画像=[[File:Tonoki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord2|34|31|16.061|N|135|27|23.504|E}}}} |所在地=[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石二丁目14番48号 | ISO = JP-27 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 16.061 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 27 | 経度秒 = 23.504 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神=[[天児屋根命]] |社格=[[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建= |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= [[10月5日]] |神事= | 地図 = Japan Osaka }} '''等乃伎神社'''（とのぎじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石にある神社。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 [[ファイル:Tonogi-jinja.jpg |サムネイル|等乃伎神社の鳥居]] == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[天児屋根命]] ; 相殿神 * [[誉田別命]] * [[菅原道真]] * [[大歳神]] * 壺大神 == 歴史 == 当神社の案内板によれば、「等乃伎」という名称はかつてこの地を収めていた[[中臣氏]]の一支族である殿来連から由来し、[[続日本紀]]の[[天平勝宝]]4年（752年）条に「中臣殿来連竹田売」という人物の名前が残っており、その頃に領地を治めていた中臣殿来連竹田売が祖先である天児屋根命を祀ったことから始まりということになっており、また同年には[[太政大臣]]である[[藤原武智麻呂]]、その子の[[大納言]]である[[恵美押勝]]（藤原仲麻呂)が相次いでこの地へ赴き、居住していたとされている [[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]とされている{{Cite web|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37?tocOpened=1|title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション『延喜式巻九・神祇九』|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。 [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）から翌年[[1909年]]（明治42年）にかけて、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を[[合祀]]した。 == 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。 従来はこの日とその前後に、[[高石だんじり祭]]のうち、氏子地域（旧富木村・旧新家村・旧土生村・旧綾井村・旧大園村）の秋祭りのだんじりが宮入りしていた。 現在は、祭りを休日に設定しようという努力により、10月の第一土曜日または第二土曜日に宮入りとなっている。 == 境内 == 本殿・拝殿は、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。 境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。境内地からは、[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。 == 境内社 == 摂社 - [[お稲荷さん|宇賀之御魂神]] 末社 - [[天御中主神]] == 交通 == *[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]下車、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600m） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * [[大阪府の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{osm box|w|562122660}} * [http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html　等乃伎神社（公式サイト）] * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai10sibu/takaisi-city/m26_02_takaisi_tonogi.html 等乃伎神社] - （大阪府神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とのきしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高石市の建築物]] [[Category:高石市の歴史]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称=等乃伎神社 |画像=[[File:Tonoki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord2|34|31|16.061|N|135|27|23.504|E}}}} |所在地=[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石二丁目14番48号 | ISO = JP-27 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 16.061 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 27 | 経度秒 = 23.504 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神=[[天児屋根命]] |社格=[[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建= |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= [[10月5日]] |神事= | 地図 = Japan Osaka }} '''等乃伎神社'''（とのぎじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石にある神社。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 [[ファイル:Tonogi-jinja.jpg |サムネイル|等乃伎神社の鳥居]] == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[天児屋根命]] ; 相殿神 * [[誉田別命]] * [[菅原道真]] * [[大歳神]] * 壺大神 == 歴史 == 当神社の案内板によれば、「等乃伎」という名称はかつてこの地を収めていた[[中臣氏]]の一支族である殿来連から由来し、[[続日本紀]]の[[天平勝宝]]4年（752年）条に「中臣殿来連竹田売」という人物の名前が残っており、その頃に領地を治めていた中臣殿来連竹田売が祖先である天児屋根命を祀ったことから始まりということになっており、また同年には[[太政大臣]]である[[藤原武智麻呂]]、その子の[[大納言]]である[[恵美押勝]]（藤原仲麻呂)が相次いでこの地へ赴き、居住していたとされているname="富木1" [[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]とされている{{Cite web|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37?tocOpened=1|title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション『延喜式巻九・神祇九』|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。 [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）から翌年[[1909年]]（明治42年）にかけて、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を[[合祀]]した。 == 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。 従来はこの日とその前後に、[[高石だんじり祭]]のうち、氏子地域（旧富木村・旧新家村・旧土生村・旧綾井村・旧大園村）の秋祭りのだんじりが宮入りしていた。 現在は、祭りを休日に設定しようという努力により、10月の第一土曜日または第二土曜日に宮入りとなっている。 == 境内 == 本殿・拝殿は、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。 境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。境内地からは、[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。 == 境内社 == 摂社 - [[お稲荷さん|宇賀之御魂神]] 末社 - [[天御中主神]] == 交通 == *[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]下車、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600m） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * [[大阪府の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{osm box|w|562122660}} * [http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html　等乃伎神社（公式サイト）] * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai10sibu/takaisi-city/m26_02_takaisi_tonogi.html 等乃伎神社] - （大阪府神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とのきしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高石市の建築物]] [[Category:高石市の歴史]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

revision.comment: [[Special:Contributions/風見草|風見草]] ([[User talk:風見草|会話]]) による 3版を[[H:RV|取り消し]] 脚注出典の破壊削除
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=等乃伎神社 |画像=[[File:Tonoki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord2|34|31|16.061|N|135|27|23.504|E}}}} |所在地=[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石二丁目14番48号 | ISO = JP-27 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 16.061 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 27 | 経度秒 = 23.504 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神=[[天児屋根命]] |社格=[[式内社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= 例祭（[[10月5日]]） |神事= | 地図 = Japan Osaka }} '''等乃伎神社'''（とのきじんじゃ、とのぎじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石にある[[神社]]{{Cite web|url=http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html|title=等乃伎神社ホームページ|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 [[ファイル:Tonogi-jinja.jpg |サムネイル|等乃伎神社の鳥居]] == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[天児屋根命]]（アマノコヤネノミコト、アメノコヤネノミコト） ; 相殿神 * [[誉田別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） * [[菅原道真]]（スガワラノミチザネ） * [[大歳神]] * 壺大神 == 歴史 == 当神社の案内板によれば、「等乃伎」という名称はかつてこの地を収めていた[[中臣氏]]の一支族である殿来連から由来し、[[続日本紀]]の[[天平勝宝]]4年（752年）条に「中臣殿来連竹田売」という人物の名前が残っており、その頃に領地を治めていた中臣殿来連竹田売が祖先である天児屋根命を祀ったことから始まりということになっており、また同年には[[太政大臣]]である[[藤原武智麻呂]]、その子の[[大納言]]である[[恵美押勝]]（藤原仲麻呂)が相次いでこの地へ赴き、居住していたとされている[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671　等乃伎神社の情報｜ 神社・お寺御朱印検索No.1／神社がいいね]2018年6月22日　閲覧。 [[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]とされている{{Cite web|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37?tocOpened=1|title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション『延喜式巻九・神祇九』|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。 [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）から翌年[[1909年]]（明治42年）にかけて、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を[[合祀]]した。 == 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。 従来はこの日とその前後に、[[高石だんじり祭]]のうち、氏子地域（旧富木村・旧新家村・旧土生村・旧綾井村・旧大園村）の秋祭りのだんじりが宮入りしていた。 現在は、祭りを休日に設定しようという努力により、10月の第一土曜日または第二土曜日に宮入りとなっている。 == 境内 == 本殿・拝殿は、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。 境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。境内地からは、[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。 == 境内社 == [[摂社]]…[[お稲荷さん|宇賀之御魂神]] [[末社]]…[[天御中主神]] == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石二丁目14番48号 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600[[メートル|m]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * [[大阪府の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{osm box|w|562122660}} * [http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html　等乃伎神社（公式サイト）] * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai10sibu/takaisi-city/m26_02_takaisi_tonogi.html 等乃伎神社] - （大阪府神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とのきしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高石市の建築物]] [[Category:高石市の歴史]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

主祭神 * [[天児屋根命]]（アマノコヤネノミコト、アメノコヤネノミコト） ; 相殿神 * [[誉田別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） * [[菅原道真]]（スガワラノミチザネ） * [[大歳神]] * 壺大神 == 歴史 == 当神社の案内板によれば、「等乃伎」という名称はかつてこの地を収めていた[[中臣氏]]の一支族である殿来連から由来し、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[天平勝宝]]4年（752年）条に「中臣殿来連竹田売」という人物の名前が残っており、その頃に領地を治めていた中臣殿来連竹田売が祖先である天児屋根命を祀ったことから始まりということになっており、また同年には[[太政大臣]]である[[藤原武智麻呂]]、その子の[[大納言]]である恵美押勝（[[藤原仲麻呂]]）が相次いでこの地へ赴き、居住していたとされている[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671　等乃伎神社の情報｜ 神社・お寺御朱印検索No.1／神社がいいね]2018年6月22日　閲覧。 [[延喜式]]の[[式内社]]とされている{{Cite web|url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37?tocOpened=1|title=国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション『延喜式巻九・神祇九』|accessdate=2018年5月29日|publisher=}}。 [[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）から翌年[[1909年]]（明治42年）にかけて、旧市場村の稲荷社、旧大園村の壺神社、旧土生村の菅原社、旧新家村の大歳社、旧南出村の大歳社を[[合祀]]した。 == 行事 == [[例祭]]は、10月5日である。 従来はこの日とその前後に、[[高石だんじり祭]]のうち、氏子地域（旧富木村・旧新家村・旧土生村・旧綾井村・旧大園村）の秋祭りのだんじりが宮入りしていた。 現在は、祭りを休日に設定しようという努力により、10月の第一土曜日または第二土曜日に宮入りとなっている。 == 境内 == 本殿・拝殿は、[[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）に建て替え、造営された。本殿は、三間社[[流造]]。拝殿は、七間社[[入母屋造]]で[[唐破風]]向拝。 境内には、[[照葉樹林]]が茂り、[[楠]]が[[神木]]である。境内地からは、[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]のものと推定される楠の焼株や、[[平安時代]]・[[鎌倉時代]]のものと推定される古瓦が出土している。 == 境内社 == [[摂社]]…[[お稲荷さん|宇賀之御魂神]] [[末社]]…[[天御中主神]] == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[大阪府]][[高石市]]取石二丁目14番48号 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[JR]][[阪和線]][[富木駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600[[メートル|m]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * [[大阪府の神社一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{osm box|w|562122660}} * [http://tonogijinja.jp/index.html　等乃伎神社（公式サイト）] * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai10sibu/takaisi-city/m26_02_takaisi_tonogi.html 等乃伎神社] - （大阪府神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とのきしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高石市の建築物]] [[Category:高石市の歴史]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system. [[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]] === Deities Enshrined === ** Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (天児屋根命, 天..."
revision.text:
== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ** Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
** Associated Deities * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami|ja|天御中主神}}
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).
=== References === {{Reflist}}
=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai10shibu/takaishi-city/10033tonogijinja.html Tonoki Shrine] - Osaka Shrine Association

== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ** Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
** Associated Deities * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. revision.text:
== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ''' Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami|ja|天御中主神}}
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).
=== References === {{Reflist}}
=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai10shibu/takaishi-city/10033tonogijinja.html Tonoki Shrine] - Osaka Shrine Association

== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ''' Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami|ja|天御中主神}}
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).
=== References === {{Reflist}}
=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai10shibu/takaishi-city/10033tonogijinja.html Tonoki Shrine] - Osaka Shrine Association

[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]

''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami|ja|天御中主神}}
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).

=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}

A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Kami|ja|天御中主神}}

== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ''' Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).

=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}

revision.text:
== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ''' Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).
=== References === {{Reflist}}
=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai10shibu/takaishi-city/10033tonogijinja.html Tonoki Shrine] - Osaka Shrine Association

revision.text:
== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ''' Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).
=== References === {{Reflist}}
=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai10shibu/takaishi-city/10033tonogijinja.html Tonoki Shrine] - Osaka Shrine Association

Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.

== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ''' Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).

=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).

=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai10shibu/takaishi-city/10033tonogijinja.html Tonoki Shrine] - Osaka Shrine Association

== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ''' Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).
=== References === {{Reflist}}
=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai10shibu/takaishi-city/10033tonogijinja.html Tonoki Shrine] - Osaka Shrine Association

In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]

== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ''' Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.

Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).

revision.text:
== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ''' Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).
=== References === {{Reflist}}
=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai10shibu/takaishi-city/10033tonogijinja.html Tonoki Shrine] - Osaka Shrine Association

revision.text:
== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ''' Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).
=== References === {{Reflist}}
=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai10shibu/takaishi-city/10033tonogijinja.html Tonoki Shrine] - Osaka Shrine Association

revision.text:
== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ''' Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).
=== References === {{Reflist}}
=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai10shibu/takaishi-city/10033tonogijinja.html Tonoki Shrine] - Osaka Shrine Association

revision.text:
== Tonoki Shrine (等乃伎神社) ==
'''Tonoki Shrine''' (等乃伎神社, also pronounced Tonogi Shrine) is a Shinto shrine located in Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and a former village shrine under the old shrine ranking system.
[[File:Tonogi-jinja.jpg|thumbnail|Torii of Tonoki Shrine]]
=== Deities Enshrined === ''' Main Deity * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (天児屋根命, 天兒屋根命)
''' Associated Deities * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別命}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Kami|ja|大歳神}} * Tsubo Daijin (壺大神)
=== History === According to the shrine's noticeboard, the name "Tonoki" derives from the Tonogai clan, a branch of the ancient {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}, who ruled this area. A record in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} mentions a person named "Nakatomi no Tonogai no Muraji Takeda-me" in the 4th year of Tenpyō Shōhō (752). The shrine is believed to have been established around that period when the Nakatomi clan worshipped their ancestor, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto. That same year, notable figures such as Fujiwara no Muchimaro, the {{ill|Daijō-daijin|ja|太政大臣}}, and his son Fujiwara no Nakamaro, also known as Emi no Oshikatsu, are said to have visited and resided in the area.<ref name="富木1">[https://jinja-bukkaku.net/detail.htm?jbId=103671 Tonoki Shrine Information - Jinja Bukkaku Search]. Retrieved June 22, 2018.</ref>
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a Shikinaisha.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Digital Collection of the National Diet Library - Engishiki, Volume 9|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537/37|accessdate=May 29, 2018}}</ref>
In 1908–1909, the shrine merged with several other local shrines, including Inari Shrine from the old Ichiba Village, Tsubo Shrine from Ōsono Village, Sugawara Shrine from Habu Village, Ōtoshi Shrine from Shinke Village, and Ōtoshi Shrine from Minamide Village.
=== Festivals and Events === The annual festival is held on October 5. Traditionally, danjiri (festival floats) from the surrounding districts would visit the shrine on this day and the days before and after. Presently, the festival has been shifted to the first or second Saturday in October for convenience, with danjiri parades taking place then.
=== Shrine Grounds === The current main and worship halls were rebuilt in 1987 (Shōwa 62). The main hall is a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with three bays, and the worship hall is a seven-bay {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gabled entrance.
The grounds feature lush broadleaf evergreen forests, with a large camphor tree serving as the sacred tree (*Shinboku*). Burnt stumps of camphor trees from the Nanboku-chō period and ancient tiles from the Heian and Kamakura periods have been excavated from the grounds.
==== Sub-Shrines ==== * {{ill|Sessha|ja|摂社}}: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀之御魂神}} * {{ill|Massha|ja|末社}}: Dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]
=== Location === * Address: 2-14-48 Toriishi, Takaishi City, Osaka Prefecture * Access: About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tōki Station|ja|富木駅}} on the JR Hanwa Line (approximately 600 meters southeast).
=== References === {{Reflist}}
=== Related Articles === {{Commonscat|Tonogi-jinja}} * {{ill|List of shrines in Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府の神社一覧}}
=== External Links === * [https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai10shibu/takaishi-city/10033tonogijinja.html Tonoki Shrine] - Osaka Shrine Association
